modules.name: horusdemodlib.horusudp
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
Horus Telemetry GUI - Horus UDP
Mark Jessop <vk5qi@rfhead.net>

functions.name: horusdemodlib.horusudp.send_payload_summary
documentation:
Send a payload summary message into the network via UDP broadcast.

Args:
telemetry (dict): Telemetry dictionary to send.
port (int): UDP port to send to.

functions.name: horusdemodlib.horusudp.send_ozimux_message
documentation:
Send an OziMux-compatible message via UDP broadcast, of the form:

TELEMETRY,HH:MM:SS,lat,lon,alt

modules.name: horusdemodlib.delegates
documentation: HorusLib - Decoder Delegate Functions

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_payload_id
documentation: Attempt to decode a payload ID into a callsign string.
Payload ID

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_time_hms
documentation:
Decode a time field, encoded as three bytes representing hours, minutes and seconds of the current UTC day.

Returns: String, as "HH:MM:SS"

Example:  -> "01:02:03"
Time representations

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_time_biseconds
documentation:
Decode a time field, encoded as a uint16, representing seconds since the start of the UTC day,
divided by 2 ('biseconds')

Returns: String, as "HH:MM:SS"

Examples: 
    0 -> 00:00:00
    1 -> 00:00:02

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_degree_float
documentation:
Convert a degree (latitude/longitude) field, provided as a float,
to a string representation, with 6 decimal places.
Latitude/Longitude representations

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_degree_fixed3
documentation:
Convert a degree (latitude/longitude) field, provided as a 
three-byte fixed-point representation, to a string.

The input is interpreted as the 3 most-significant-bytes of a
little-endian 4-byte signed integer. The LSB is set to 0x00.

Once converted to an int, the value is then scaled to degrees by
multilying by 1e-7.

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_battery_5v_byte
documentation:
Decode a battery voltage, encoded as as a single byte, where
0 = 0v, 255 = 5.0V, with linear steps in between.

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_divide_by_10
documentation: Accepts an fixed-point integer, and returns it as its value divided by 10, as a string.

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_divide_by_100
documentation: Accepts an fixed-point integer, and returns it as its value divided by 100, as a string.

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_field
documentation: Attempt to decode a field, supplied as bytes, using a specified delegate function

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.decode_custom_fields
documentation: Attempt to decode custom field data from the 9-byte custom section of a 32-byte payload

functions.name: horusdemodlib.delegates.fix_datetime
documentation: Given a HH:MM:SS string from a telemetry sentence, produce a complete timestamp, using the current system time as a guide for the date.

modules.name: horusdemodlib.checksums
documentation: HorusLib - Checksumming functions

functions.name: horusdemodlib.checksums.ukhas_crc
documentation:
Calculate the CRC16 CCITT checksum of *data*.

(CRC16 CCITT: start 0xFFFF, poly 0x1021)

functions.name: horusdemodlib.checksums.check_packet_crc
documentation:
Attempt to validate a packets checksum, which is assumed to be present
in the last few bytes of the packet.

Support CRC types: CRC16-CCITT

functions.name: horusdemodlib.checksums.add_packet_crc
documentation:
Add a CRC onto the end of provided bytes

Support CRC types: CRC16-CCITT

modules.name: horusdemodlib.sondehubamateur
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
HorusDemodLib - SondeHub Amateur Uploader
Uploads telemetry to the SondeHub ElasticSearch cluster,
in the new 'universal' format descried here:
https://github.com/projecthorus/sondehub-infra/wiki/%5BDRAFT%5D-Amateur-Balloon-Telemetry-Format
Copyright (C) 2022  Mark Jessop <vk5qi@rfhead.net>
Released under GNU GPL v3 or later

documentation:
Initialise and start a Sondehub (Amateur) uploader

Args:
    upload_rate (int): How often to upload batches of data.
    upload_timeout (int): Upload timeout.

Upload a station position packet to SondeHub.

This uses the PUT /listeners API described here:

Check if the uploader thread is running.

Helper function to log a debug message with a descriptive heading.

documentation:
Helper function to log a warning message with a descriptive heading.

Sondehub (Amateur) Uploader Class.

Accepts telemetry dictionaries from a decoder, buffers them up, and then compresses and uploads
them to the Sondehub Elasticsearch cluster.

modules.name: horusdemodlib.encoder
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
HorusDemodLib - Encoder helper functions

classes.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder

methods.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
----------
libpath : str
    Path to libhorus

methods.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder.__enter__
documentation: in case someone wanted to use `with` style. I'm not sure if closing the modem does a lot.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder.close
documentation: Closes Horus modem. Does nothing here.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder.get_num_tx_data_bytes
documentation:
Calculate the number of transmit data bytes (uw+packet+fec) for a given
input packet size.
Wrappers for libhorus C functions that we need.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder.horus_l2_encode_packet
documentation:
Encode a packet using the Horus Binary FEC scheme, which:
- Generates Golay (23,12) FEC for the packet
- Adds this onto the packet contents, and interleaves/scrambles
- Adds a unique word (0x2424) to the start.

Packet input must be provided as bytes.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder.horus_l2_decode_packet
documentation:
Decode a Horus-Binary encoded data packet.

The packet must be provided as bytes, and must have the 2-byte unique word (0x2424)
at the start.

The expected number of output bytes must also be provided (22 or 32 for Horus v1 / v2 respectively)

methods.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder.bytes_to_4fsk_symbols
documentation:
Convert a sequence of bytes to a sequence of 4FSK symbols (0,1,2,3) for modulation.
Also adds a preamble sequence, by default 8 bytes long.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder.bytes_to_onebitperbyte
documentation:
Convert a sequence of bytes to a one-bit-per-yte sequence, for modulation
using fsk_mod.
Also adds a preamble sequence, by default 8 bytes long.

This data, if written out to a file, can be encoded using the fsk_mod utility, e.g.
./fsk_mod 4 48000 100 1000 270 onebitperbyte.bin test4fsk.raw
produces a 48000 Hz Sample rate, Signed 16-bit output.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.encoder.Encoder.create_horus_v2_packet
documentation: Create and encode a Horus V2 Data Packet.

Horus Binary Encoder class.

Allows creation of a Horus Binary packet.

modules.name: horusdemodlib.demodstats
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
Horus Binary - fsk_demod modem statistics parser
Copyright (C) 2019  Mark Jessop <vk5qi@rfhead.net>
Released under GNU GPL v3 or later
This utility ingests fsk_demod stats output via stdin, and optionally emits time-averaged modem statistics
data via UDP.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demodstats.FSKDemodStats.__init__
documentation:
Required Fields:
    averaging_time (float): Use the last X seconds of data in calculations.
    peak_hold (bool): If true, use a peak-hold SNR metric instead of a mean.
    decoder_id (str): A unique ID for this object (suggest use of the SDR device ID)

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demodstats.FSKDemodStats.update
documentation:
Update the statistics parser with a new set of output from fsk_demod.
This can accept either a string (which will be parsed as JSON), or a dict.

Required Fields:
    data (str, dict): One set of statistics from fsk_demod.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demodstats.FSKDemodStats.log_error
documentation:
Helper function to log an error message with a descriptive heading.

Process modem statistics produced by horus/fsk_demod and provide access to
filtered or instantaneous modem data.

This class expects the JSON output from horus_demod to be arriving in *realtime*.
The test script below will emulate relatime input based on a file.

modules.name: horusdemodlib.demod
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
HorusDemodLib - LibHorus Wrapper Class

classes.name: horusdemodlib.demod.COMP
documentation: Used in MODEM_STATS for representing IQ.

classes.name: horusdemodlib.demod.MODEM_STATS
documentation: Extended modem stats structure

classes.name: horusdemodlib.demod.Mode
documentation: Modes (and aliases for modes) for the HorusLib modem

classes.name: horusdemodlib.demod.Frame
documentation:
Frame class used for demodulation attempts. 

Attributes
----------

data : bytes
    Demodulated data output. Empty if demodulation didn't succeed
sync : bool
    Modem sync status
snr : float
    Estimated SNR
crc_pass : bool
    CRC check status
extended_stats
    Extended modem stats. These are provided as c_types so will need to be cast prior to use. See MODEM_STATS for structure details

classes.name: horusdemodlib.demod.HorusLib

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demod.HorusLib.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
----------
libpath : str
    Path to libhorus
mode : Mode
    horuslib.Mode.BINARY, horuslib.Mode.BINARY_V2_256BIT, horuslib.Mode.BINARY_V2_128BIT, horuslib.Mode.RTTY, RTTY_7N2 = 99
rate : int
    Changes the modem rate for supported modems. -1 for default
tone_spacing : int
    Spacing between tones (hz) -1 for default
stereo_iq : bool
    use stereo (IQ) input (quadrature)
verbose : bool
    Enabled horus_set_verbose
callback : function
    When set you can use add_samples to add any number of audio frames and callback will be called when a demodulated frame is avaliable.
sample_rate : int
    The input sample rate of the audio input

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demod.HorusLib.__enter__
documentation: in case someone wanted to use `with` style. I'm not sure if closing the modem does a lot.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demod.HorusLib.close
documentation: Closes Horus modem.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demod.HorusLib._update_nin
documentation: Updates nin. Called every time RF is demodulated and doesn't need to be run manually

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demod.HorusLib.demodulate
documentation:
Demodulates audio in, into bytes output.

Parameters
----------
demod_in : bytes
    16bit, signed for audio in. You'll need .nin frames in to work correctly.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demod.HorusLib.set_estimator_limits
documentation: Update the modems internal frequency estimator limits

methods.name: horusdemodlib.demod.HorusLib.add_samples
documentation: Add samples to a input buffer, to pass on to demodulate when we have nin samples

HorusLib provides a binding to horuslib to demoulate frames.

Example usage:

from horuslib import HorusLib, Mode
with HorusLib(, mode=Mode.BINARY, verbose=False) as horus:
    with open("test.wav", "rb") as f:
        while True:
            data = f.read(horus.nin*2)
            if horus.nin != 0 and data == b'': #detect end of file
                break
            output = horus.demodulate(data)
            if output.crc_pass and output.data:
                print(f'{output.data.hex()} SNR: {output.snr}')
                for x in range(horus.mfsk):
                    print(f'F{str(x)}: {float(output.extended_stats.f_est[x])}')

modules.name: horusdemodlib.habitat
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
Horus Demod Library - Habitat Uploader
Mark Jessop <vk5qi@rfhead.net>

documentation: Handle uploading of packets to Habitat

documentation: Add a sentence to the upload queue

documentation: Shutdown uploader thread.

methods.name: horusdemodlib.habitat.HabitatUploader.uploadListenerPosition
documentation: Initializer Listener Callsign, and upload Listener Position

methods.name: horusdemodlib.habitat.HabitatUploader.trigger_position_upload
documentation: Trigger a re-upload of the listener position

Queued Habitat Telemetry Uploader class 

Packets to be uploaded to Habitat are added to a queue for uploading.
If an upload attempt times out, the packet is discarded.
If the queue fills up (probably indicating no network connection, and a fast packet downlink rate),
it is immediately emptied, to avoid upload of out-of-date packets.
